LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ROSA FORNESI, AND, ENRICO FORNESI DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST IDENTIFY WHICH CLAIMS ARE ASSERTED AGAINST WHICH DEFENDANT. (SEE CRC 2.112.) PLAINTIFF MUST PARTICULARLY PLEAD ANY AGENCY RELATIONSHIP BETWEEN THE DEFENDANTS, AND THAT THE AGENT'S DUTIES AROSE OUT OF A RELATIONSHIP UNIQUE TO THE TENANT AND LANDLORD. CIVIL CODE SECTION 1941 APPLIES TO "LESSORS" OF PROPERTY. NO FACTS PLED REGARDING WHAT KIND OF STATUTORY OR COMMON LAW DUTY TO PLAINTIFF AROSE TO SUPPORT THE 7TH CAUSE OF ACTION. THE MOTION TO STRIKE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS DENIED. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Off Calendar Jul-31-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ROSA FORNESI, and ENRICO FORNESI MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Appearances: James M. Morris, Esq. of Parish & Small (209) 952-1992, and William. S. Weisberg, Esq. of Weisberg & Miller (415) 296-7070, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; John M. Ingle, Esq. of Michel & Fackler (925) 933-7309, appearing for Defendants and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Adjudication is denied as moot. Plaintiff filed her second amended complaint on July 14, 2009. An amended complaint supercedes the original and furnishes the sole basis for the cause of action. The original complaint is dropped out of the case and ceases to have any effect as a pleading, or as a basis for a judgment. Anmaco, Inc. vs. Bohlken (1993) 13 Cal.App.4th 891, 901. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

(302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-20-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROSA FORNESI, and, ENRICO FORNESI DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer is sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff must identify which claims are asserted against which defendant. (See CRC 2.112.) Plaintiff must particularly plead any agency relationship between the defendants, and that the agent's duties arose out of a relationship unique to the tenant and landlord. Civil Code section 1941 applies to "lessors" of property. No facts pled regarding what kind of statutory or common law duty to Plaintiff arose to support the 7th Cause of Action. The motion to strike punitive damages claim is denied. (302/CWW/JW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759

After hearing, Plaintiff's Motion to Compel IME is granted.
Plaintiff to submit to IME at earliest date convenient for plaintiff, her counsel and Examining doctor.
Examination may include spirometry but not blood testing until ordered by the court; approprieate medical history may be obtained.
Plaintiff may be required to partially disrobe for examination, but may be provided gown or drape to protect privacy.
Requests for sanctions denied without prejudice.
